name: Cree
language: Cree (various dialects: Y-dialect, TH-dialect, N-dialect, L-dialect; Innu-aimun is closely related)
traditionalTerritories: One of the largest traditional territories of any First Nation in North America, spanning from northern Quebec and Labrador west to Alberta and into the Northwest Territories. Includes the boreal forest, subarctic transition zones, and the northern prairies. Eeyou Istchee (Cree of northern Quebec) is a significant contemporary territory.
culturalNotes: The Cree are one of the largest First Nations peoples in Canada. 'Cree' encompasses several distinct but related nations. The Eeyou of northern Quebec have strong contemporary governance through the Cree Nation Government. Plains Cree were historically buffalo hunters, while Woodland/Swampy Cree peoples are hunters, trappers, and fishers of the boreal forest. Métis culture (French-Canadian and Cree intermarriage) shares many Cree heritage skills. Cree oral tradition includes the Wihtikow (windigo) stories, the trickster Wisahkecahk, and extensive knowledge of northern ecology.
heritageSkillRelevance: Pemmican preparation — lean caribou or bison meat, rendered fat, dried berries; the definitive portable food of the northern fur trade era
Birchbark canoe construction (different regional variants from Anishinaabe styles)
Snowshoe construction — multiple styles for different terrain (boreal forest vs. open terrain)
Beadwork in floral style on moose hide and cloth (Cree/Métis floral beadwork is regionally distinctive)
Rabbit snaring and small game trapping
Fish smoking and drying (particularly whitefish and pike)
Bannock preparation (historically adapted from Scottish/European introduction, now a Cree tradition)
Northern plant medicine — knowledge of boreal forest medicinal plants
communityAuthorizedResources: Aanischaaukamikw Cree Cultural Institute — creeculture.ca — Eeyou Istchee, Quebec
Cree Nation Government — cngov.ca — Eeyou Istchee
Pimicikamak Cree Nation cultural programs — Manitoba
culturalSovereigntyNotes: Pemmican preparation (general techniques) is Level 1 with Cree attribution. Specific ceremonial protocols around first harvest or first kill are Level 2. Sweat lodge ceremony protocols are Level 4 — hard block, never include details.
